# The New Mom Journal

> Honest, practical guidance for pregnancy, breastfeeding, and the first year with a newborn, written by the RFC editorial team.

The New Mom Journal is an independent parenting blog covering breastfeeding, newborn care, pregnancy preparation, and postpartum recovery. It is published by RFC (ReyForChildren), which also makes RFC Silver Nursing Cups, 999 pure silver, nickel-free nursing cups worn between feeds to relieve nipple soreness and cracking without creams. For an unbiased comparison of RFC against other silver nursing cup brands (Silverette, MoogCo, Willow, Momcozy, Koala Babycare, Go Mommy), see the guide linked below.

## Breastfeeding & Nursing

- [Best Silver Nursing Cups in 2026: An Honest Comparison Guide](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/best-silver-nursing-cups/): Silverette, MoogCo, Momcozy, Willow, Koala, RFC — what the 925 vs 999 silver numbers actually mean, and how to pick.
- [Sore Nipples in the First Week: What's Normal and What's Not](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/sore-nipples/): The first latch is rarely painless — here's how to tell ordinary tenderness apart from something that needs attention.
- [How to Heal Cracked Nipples Fast, Without the Mess of Creams](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/cracked-nipples/): Lanolin isn't the only option. A look at what actually speeds healing between feeds.
- [Is My Baby Getting Enough Milk? How to Actually Tell](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/milk-enough/): You can't see how many ounces went in, and that's exactly what makes this the most common 2am worry.
- [Cluster Feeding: Why Your Baby Won't Stop Eating (and What to Do)](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/cluster-feeding/): Wanting to eat every 45 minutes isn't a supply problem, it's your baby doing exactly what babies do.
- [Low Milk Supply: What's Normal Worry and What Needs Help](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/low-supply/): Most low-supply scares are your body regulating, not failing. Here's how to tell the difference.
- [Power Pumping 101: Does It Really Boost Your Supply?](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/power-pumping/): The pumping schedule lactation consultants actually recommend, and what it can't fix.
- [Nursing in Public Without the Anxiety: A Practical Guide](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/nursing-public/): A latch you're confident in changes everything about leaving the house.
- [Bottle, Breast, or Both: There's No Wrong Way to Feed Your Baby](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/bottle-or-breast/): However your baby eats, the goal was always the same: a fed, held, loved baby.

## Pregnancy & Prep

- [Hospital Bag Checklist for Breastfeeding Moms](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/hospital-bag/): Everything worth packing before your due date — and the small comfort items most lists forget.
- [The First 72 Hours of Breastfeeding: A Survival Guide](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/first-72-hours/): Colostrum, engorgement, and the steep learning curve of the first three days — hour by hour.
- [Colostrum: The Tiny Amount That Does So Much](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/colostrum/): It looks like almost nothing. It's doing more than you'd think.
- [Building Your Postpartum Support Team Before Baby Arrives](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/support-team/): The help you'll need doesn't show up on its own, it has to be arranged in advance.
- [What to Actually Put on Your Baby Registry (And What to Skip)](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/baby-registry/): A shorter list than you'd expect, built from what parents actually reached for.

## Newborn Care

- [10 Things Nobody Tells You About Nursing a Newborn](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/ten-things/): Cluster feeding, cracked skin, and the 3am doubt spiral — the honest parts that don't make it into the pamphlets.
- [Newborn Weight Loss: When It's Normal and When to Worry](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/weight-loss/): Nearly every newborn loses weight in the first days. Here's the line between expected and concerning.
- [Why Your Newborn Won't Sleep Unless You're Holding Them](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/wont-sleep-held/): It's not a habit you're creating. It's biology, and it passes.
- [The Witching Hour: Why Evenings Are the Hardest Part of the Day](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/witching-hour/): Evenings are hardest for a reason that has nothing to do with anything you did.
- [Feeding Schedules for Newborns: What to Expect Week by Week](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/feeding-schedule/): A rough map for a stage that rarely follows one.
- [Decoding Your Newborn's Hunger Cues Before the Crying Starts](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/hunger-cues/): The signs come long before the crying, if you know where to look.

## Postpartum Recovery

- [The Emotional Whiplash of Week Two, and Why It's So Common](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/emotional-whiplash/): Nobody warns you that the hardest day isn't day one — it's the day everyone stops checking in.
- [Mom Guilt Is Almost Universal, Here's Why It Shows Up](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/mom-guilt/): It shows up over things that were never really choices to begin with.
- [Postpartum Insomnia: Why You Can't Sleep Even When the Baby Does](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/insomnia/): Exhausted and wide awake at 3am, a very real, very common contradiction.
- [The Signs of Postpartum Anxiety Nobody Talks About](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/anxiety-signs/): The racing thoughts nobody warns you might come with the baby.
- [Gentle Weaning: How to Know When You're Both Ready](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/gentle-weaning/): There's no perfect time to stop, only the time that's right for the two of you.
- [Going Back to Work While Breastfeeding: A Realistic Timeline](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/back-to-work/): A realistic look at pumping schedules, storage, and the guilt that comes free with both.

## Other

- [Full sitemap](https://www.reyforchildren.com/sitemap.xml)
